pathophysiology_summary: Post-viral fatigue is prolonged, disproportionate exhaustion following an infection, characterised by unrefreshing sleep, post-exertional malaise (delayed, prolonged symptom exacerbation after minor physical/cognitive effort), myalgia/arthralgia and 'brain fog', without an alternative organic explanation; it is a diagnosis of exclusion and typically improves over weeks to months. The ground-truth task is (1) to actively exclude the dangerous and treatable causes of new profound fatigue after a viral illness — especially post-viral myocarditis (chest pain, breathlessness, palpitations, oedema, exertional syncope — which telehealth CANNOT exclude and warrants an ECG at minimum), plus anaemia, thyroid dysfunction, occult infection and malignancy (weight loss, night sweats, fever, lymphadenopathy, focal deficits) — by arranging baseline bloods, and (2) to manage supportively with activity pacing (avoiding the push-crash cycle) rather than pushing through or relying on caffeine, with a clear cardiac safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Profound post-infectious fatigue with unrefreshing sleep and post-exertional malaise.
New fatigue/exertional intolerance after a virus can be myocarditis — screen chest pain/dyspnoea/palpitations/oedema/syncope; needs ECG.

differential.diagnosis: Anaemia / thyroid dysfunction / occult infection / malignancy
differential.evidence_basis: Treatable organic causes of fatigue — screen red flags and arrange baseline bloods.
Consider mood; the clear viral trigger and post-exertional crashes with preserved desire for activity favour post-viral fatigue.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Post-viral fatigue may lead, but myocarditis and organic causes must be actively excluded (history + bloods ± ECG) before the label is applied.

differential.evidence_basis: No cardiac features, no sinister organic red flags, classic post-exertional-malaise pattern after a virus — post-viral fatigue pending normal baseline bloods/ECG.
Cannot be fully excluded remotely — retained as a cardiac safety-net; an ECG is prudent and any cardiac feature mandates emergency assessment.
No red flags; to be excluded by baseline bloods.

differential_progression.scoring_note: After a negative red-flag screen and baseline investigations, post-viral fatigue leads; supportive pacing with a cardiac safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis of exclusion; non-linear recovery over weeks–months with pacing; reassess if red flags or abnormal bloods.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final position is post-viral fatigue (diagnosis of exclusion).

red_flags.description: Myocarditis / cardiac features — chest pain, breathlessness (especially at rest or worsening on exertion), palpitations, ankle oedema, exertional syncope
red_flags.clinical_significance: Post-viral myocarditis is potentially life-threatening and cannot be excluded via telehealth — needs ECG ± troponin/echo.

red_flags.description: Sinister organic red flags — unexplained weight loss, drenching night sweats, persistent fever, lymphadenopathy, focal neurological deficit, bleeding

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Applying 'post-viral fatigue' without screening cardiac features / arranging baseline bloods (it is a diagnosis of exclusion).

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Advising 'push through' / graded exercise as a cure
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Encouraging pushing through post-exertional malaise worsens crashes; pacing is preferred.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, iron studies, CRP)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Arrange baseline bloods ± ECG; supportive pacing; 000/ED for cardiac features; GP for organic red flags.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Missing myocarditis or a treatable organic cause behind 'post-viral fatigue'.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: New significant fatigue warrants a history, examination and baseline investigations (FBC, iron studies, TFTs, U&E, LFTs, CRP, glucose) to exclude organic causes. Post-viral fatigue is a diagnosis of exclusion managed with activity pacing; investigate/escalate red flags (cardiac, weight loss, night sweats, focal signs).
